item.title: After the highs of Barcelona, Lewis Hamilton and Ferrari were 'hit with reality' in Austria
item.description: Lewis Hamilton went from the high of winning his first grand prix for Ferrari earlier this month to a fifth-place finish in Austria Sunday

item.title: Is George Russell really back? Plus: How F1’s new drone footage works
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Claire Williams was the last woman to run an F1 team. Why hasn’t there been another since?
item.description: Claire Williams left the paddock as a team boss in 2020 and says she is now on the 'periphery' of the sport

item.title: Max Verstappen's F1 future is back in the spotlight. Could he really go to McLaren?
item.description: Max Verstappen's future with Red Bull is once again dominating discussions in the paddock

item.title: The Max Verstappen factor that made Red Bull Mercedes' biggest threat in Austria
item.description: Mercedes won again in Austria, but Max Verstappen produced a starring drive that got Red Bull back towards its best.

item.title: Austrian GP: Submit your questions for our F1 mailbag
item.description: What’s on your mind after the 2026 Austrian Grand Prix? Send us your questions and we’ll answer

item.title: Austrian Grand Prix briefing: Russell wins, Verstappen impresses in exhilarating race
item.description: George Russell wins for the first time since the opening weekend of the season in a race full of overtaking and intriguing strategy calls.

item.title: Why George Russell keeps controversial pole at 2026 Austrian GP after Max Verstappen crash
item.description: The Mercedes driver had been set to go quickest before Max Verstappen crashed, as yellow flag drama confuses post-qualifying celebrations

item.title: Mercedes thought it had the 2026 F1 title battle all to itself. Not anymore
item.description: Mercedes dominated the start of the 2026 F1 season but lost the last race to Ferrari. Why as it suddenly lost form?

item.title: Questions about Lewis Hamilton's hushed injury. Plus: Kimi Antonelli leads early in Austria
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Red Bull at 'large risk' of being unable to upgrade F1 engine if review results stand, says team principal
item.description: Laurent Mekies was coy on Max Verstappen's F1 future during a Friday press conference which centred on engine upgrades.

item.title: World Cup goals, 800m landmarks and Tour de France wins: Famous records that may soon be broken
item.description: Our writers explore several notable sporting milestones that could potentially be eclipsed in the near future

item.title: What we're hearing about F1's 2027 driver market - including Max Verstappen and Fernando Alonso
item.description: F1's 2027 driver market is yet to catch alight, but that could all change if Max Verstappen and Fernando Alonso choose to make moves

item.title: Austrian GP track breakdown: F1’s mountain sprint at the mighty Red Bull Ring
item.description: The Austrian circuit features 2.7 miles of steep elevation changes, long straights, all kinds of corners — and track limits concerns.

item.title: Lewis Hamilton says he suffered serious neck injury during Ferrari testing in 2025
item.description: Hamilton said he "couldn't do a lot for nine weeks" after a crash in testing.

item.title: FIA removes presidency term limit rules after general assembly vote
item.description: The move allows president Mohammed Ben Sulayem to stay in his role until he is defeated in an election or opts against running for again.

item.title: How F1 will cope with Europe’s heat wave in Austria: ‘Space jackets’ and rule tweaks
item.description: The paddock is preparing itself for a sweltering return to action as a record-breaking heat wave passes through Europe.

item.title: The Spanish Civil War bunkers F1's newest track was forced to avoid
item.description: The Athletic tours the Madring in Spain, which in three months will host its first Spanish Grand Prix

item.title: F1 desperately needs a drama king. Plus: Check out the newest, raddest corner
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Lewis Hamilton’s F1 revival: The training 'mission', a new engineer and Kim Kardashian
item.description: After his tough start with Ferrari in 2025, Lewis Hamilton doesn't put his string of strong performances this year down to one change.

item.title: Mercedes withdraws petition on Pierre Gasly's Monaco penalties after "discussion with FIA and F1"
item.description: Mercedes may have dropped its right of review petition, but the appeals lodged by McLaren and Red Bull are still moving forward.

item.title: F1 mailbag: Has Ferrari got the fastest car? Has Mercedes got a driver dilemma?
item.description: After Lewis Hamilton's statement win in Barcelona, our experts answer your post-race questions.

item.title: Mercedes receives FIA hearing date over controversial Monaco GP penalty situation
item.description: Pierre Gasly had his five-second penalty rescinded following a right of review request by Alpine, reinstating his third-place finish.

item.title: What Qatar GP date firmness means for F1 rescheduling canceled Bahrain or Saudi Arabia races
item.description: The Bahrain and Saudi Arabian GPs were both scheduled to be held in April but were canceled due to the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Finally, a non-Mercedes winner! Plus: A love letter to Le Mans
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Welcome to F1's new era of 'speed chess,' where every pass comes with a price
item.description: DRS is out. Overtake Mode is in. Here's why drivers say passing just got a lot harder

item.title: Why F1's boiling drama over engine compression ratios matters for the 2026 season
item.description: Has Mercedes found a loophole with its engines? And what exactly is the loophole, anyway?

item.title: F1's new cars behave unlike any others. It's all down to new aerodynamics
item.description: F1 has new cars in 2026, which look different to previous eras in grand prix racing. Here's how they work and what the drivers think

item.title: F1 hits reset in 2026. Your guide to the new cars, new rules and new era
item.description: Formula 1 evolves dramatically in 2026. Here's your year-long guide to it all

item.title: F1's new engines are the sport's biggest gamble in 12 years. Here's what changed
item.description: F1 is introducing new hybrid engines ahead of the 2026 season. Here's how they work and why they matter

item.title: How Formula 1's cost cap works: Rules, penalties and past breaches explained
item.description: The sport's $135 million spending limit aims to create parity — but compliance isn't always simple

item.title: F1 penalty points: How they work and why they're needed even though drivers have doubts
item.description: A deep dive into the details - and arguments - of Formula One penalty points for driver infractions

item.title: What are F1 driver development programs? Inside the academies that nurture future champions
item.description: All of F1's new 2025 drivers have one thing in common - they were part of a team’s driver development program or academy.

item.title: What are F1’s racing rules? A look at race stewards and drivers' battle for clarity
item.description: Multiple times during the 2024 season, drivers have faced questions — and raised their own — about what is and is not allowed on track.

item.title: How F1's Red Bull mastered the art of the 2-second pit stop
item.description: A deep dive into the positions, the practice, and the fine-tuned production that is the Formula One pit stop.

item.title: Welcome to Between the Racing Lines, your new guide to all things F1
item.description: F1 is complicated and confusing at times, and is constantly evolving. Here's your guide to keeping up.

item.title: Sim time, data crunching and Candy Crush: The life of an F1 reserve driver
item.description: Reserve drivers are more than simple substitutes — they play important roles within their teams, even if they rarely get to hit the track.

item.title: How F1 drivers train physically and mentally to go from 'gym fit' to 'race fit'
item.description: To have a chance at the title, drivers need not just a top-caliber car but to extract the maximum performance from it — and themselves.
